_comment: Short, plain remediation tips keyed by engine rule id. Shown in bug reports alongside the engine's own help URL. Add tips here as you triage common findings; unknown rules fall back to the help URL. Keys are axe rule ids (e.g. 'color-contrast') and Siteimprove Alfa rule ids (e.g. 'sia-r2').

axe.image-alt: Give every a meaningful alt attribute. Use alt="" only for decorative images so screen readers skip them.
axe.role-img-alt: Elements with role="img" need an accessible name via aria-label or aria-labelledby.
axe.color-contrast: Increase the contrast between text and its background to at least 4.5:1 (3:1 for large text). Check both light and dark modes.
axe.color-contrast-enhanced: For AAA, text needs 7:1 contrast (4.5:1 for large text).
axe.link-name: Give the link discernible text. Avoid empty links and icon-only links without an aria-label.
axe.button-name: Give the button an accessible name via its text content, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.
axe.label: Associate every form control with a <label> (via for/id) or an aria-label/aria-labelledby.
axe.form-field-multiple-labels: A form field should have exactly one label; remove the duplicates.
axe.heading-order: Use heading levels in order (h1 → h2 → h3) without skipping; structure, not size.
axe.empty-heading: Remove empty headings or give them text. Don't use heading tags for spacing.
axe.landmark-one-main: Each page should have exactly one <main> landmark.
axe.landmark-banner-is-top-level: Put the banner landmark (<header>) at the top level, not nested inside another landmark.
axe.region: Wrap all page content in landmarks (header, nav, main, footer) so users can navigate by region.
axe.list: Use <ul>/<ol> with only <li> children for lists; don't fake lists with <div>s.
axe.aria-required-attr: Add the ARIA attributes this role requires (e.g. aria-checked on a checkbox role).
axe.aria-valid-attr-value: Fix the ARIA attribute value — it must reference a real id or use an allowed value.
axe.aria-allowed-attr: Remove ARIA attributes that aren't allowed on this role/element.
axe.aria-hidden-focus: Don't put focusable elements inside aria-hidden="true"; they become a keyboard trap for screen-reader users.
axe.duplicate-id: Make every id unique on the page; duplicate ids break label and ARIA associations.
axe.duplicate-id-active: Active/focusable elements must have unique ids.
axe.html-has-lang: Add a lang attribute to <html> (e.g. lang="en").
axe.html-lang-valid: Use a valid BCP-47 language code in the lang attribute.
axe.document-title: Give the page a unique, descriptive <title>.
axe.frame-title: Give every <iframe> a descriptive title attribute.
axe.tabindex: Avoid tabindex values greater than 0; they create a confusing tab order.
axe.scrollable-region-focusable: Make scrollable regions keyboard-focusable (tabindex="0") so keyboard users can scroll them.
axe.meta-viewport: Don't disable zoom (user-scalable=no / maximum-scale=1); low-vision users need to zoom.
axe.td-headers-attr: Associate data cells with their headers correctly using headers/id or scope.
axe.th-has-data-cells: Ensure table headers actually describe data cells; use scope on <th>.
axe.label-content-name-mismatch: The accessible name should contain the visible label text so voice-control users can target it.

alfa.sia-r2: Give every an accessible name (alt text), or alt="" if purely decorative.
alfa.sia-r11: Give the link an accessible name via text content or aria-label.
alfa.sia-r12: Give the button an accessible name via text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.
alfa.sia-r13: Give the <iframe> an accessible name (title attribute).
alfa.sia-r8: Associate the form field with a label.
alfa.sia-r66: Increase text contrast to meet WCAG AAA (7:1).
alfa.sia-r69: Increase text contrast to meet WCAG AA (4.5:1, or 3:1 for large text).
alfa.sia-r16: Add the states and properties this ARIA role requires.
alfa.sia-r18: Only use ARIA states/properties that are allowed on this element/role.
alfa.sia-r4: Add a valid lang attribute to <html>.
alfa.sia-r53: Use headings in a logical, non-skipping order.
alfa.sia-r62: Make links visually distinguishable from surrounding text (not by color alone).
